evals.prompt: Set up a claude.ai project for a new client — Rosewood Lettings, an estate agent and letting agency in Clapham, South London. Owner is Tom Ashford, Managing Director. They handle residential sales and lettings across SW London. Been trading 12 years, known for premium service and local expertise. Typical customer: professionals relocating, landlords with 3-10 properties, families upsizing. Brand personality YES: premium, knowledgeable, trustworthy. Brand personality NO: aggressive, cheap, impersonal. Colours: #1A3C5E (navy), #C9A96E (gold), #FFFFFF (white). Fonts: not specified. Tone: polished and professional, confident without being arrogant. Always use: "your property", "local expertise", "dedicated service". Never use: "bargain", "cheap", "won't last long". Email sign-off: "Kind regards, Tom". Pain points: 1) property descriptions are inconsistent across agents, 2) vendor update emails are ad hoc, 3) social media is sporadic. They want ONE combined Project. Goals: grow lettings portfolio, improve brand consistency. Tech comfort: moderate. Device: Mac.
evals.expected_output: Complete output folder at /Users/wayne/Claude/client-brains/rosewood-lettings/ with project-instructions.md (under 8000 chars), setup-guide.md, qa-report.md, and /knowledge/ folder with brand-summary.md and relevant templates. Estate agent terminology and compliance (Property Misdescriptions Act, EPC, TPO). British English throughout.

assertions.text: Output folder /client-brains/rosewood-lettings/ contains project-instructions.md, setup-guide.md, qa-report.md, and knowledge/ folder with brand-summary.md
project-instructions.md is under 8,000 characters
No {{PLACEHOLDER}} or [PLACEHOLDER] values remain in any output file
Business name 'Rosewood Lettings' appears consistently across all files (not misspelled or varied)
British English throughout — no -ize spellings, 'color', 'center', etc.
Uses estate agent terminology correctly (e.g., 'vendor' not 'seller', 'property' not 'house', EPC mentioned in compliance)
Project instructions reflect the client's brand voice (premium, knowledgeable, trustworthy) and banned words are excluded
Setup guide includes 3-5 example prompts tailored to estate agent use cases (property descriptions, vendor updates, social media)
QA report includes all must-pass checks and an assumptions log listing any industry defaults used
Only one project-instructions.md file generated (not split into multiple Projects)

evals.prompt: claude.ai onboarding for a coach — Elevate Coaching, run by Nadia Hussain from her home office in Reading. She's a leadership and executive coach, works with mid-career professionals and team leads who feel stuck. Brand personality YES: empowering, honest, warm. Brand personality NO: corporate, salesy, preachy. No brand colours or fonts. Tone: conversational but credible — like a supportive mentor. Always use: "your growth", "real progress", "let's work on this". Never use: "synergy", "leverage", "circle back". Email sign-off: "Warmly, Nadia". Pain points: 1) writing follow-up emails after discovery calls takes ages, 2) blog content is inconsistent, 3) LinkedIn posts feel forced. She wants Claude to help with emails, blog posts, and LinkedIn content. Single Project is fine. Goals: get 3 new corporate clients this quarter, build thought leadership. Tech comfort: low — first time using Claude. Device: iPad.
evals.expected_output: Complete output folder with project-instructions.md under 8000 chars, setup guide written for a non-technical user on iPad, knowledge files including brand summary and email templates. Coaching terminology, no compliance notes needed. QA report with assumptions log noting missing colours/fonts.

assertions.text: Output folder /client-brains/elevate-coaching/ contains project-instructions.md, setup-guide.md, qa-report.md, and knowledge/ folder with brand-summary.md
project-instructions.md is under 8,000 characters
No {{PLACEHOLDER}} or [PLACEHOLDER] values remain in any output file
QA report assumptions log flags missing brand colours and fonts as ASSUMED or MISSING
Setup guide is written for a non-technical user — no jargon, step-by-step instructions, mentions iPad where relevant
Project instructions reflect conversational, mentor-like tone — not corporate
None of the banned words (synergy, leverage, circle back) appear in project instructions
Knowledge folder contains email templates and/or content templates relevant to coaching use cases
Setup guide includes example prompts for follow-up emails, blog posts, and LinkedIn content

evals.prompt: create project instructions for a solicitor — Blackstone & Hart, a law firm in Birmingham. Senior Partner is Catherine Hart. They do commercial property, employment law, and dispute resolution. 6 solicitors plus 3 support staff. Typical clients: SMEs, property developers, HR departments. Brand personality YES: authoritative, precise, reliable. Brand personality NO: casual, trendy, vague. Colours: #2C2C2C (charcoal), #8B0000 (burgundy), #F5F5F5 (off-white). Fonts: Georgia for headings, system sans for body. Tone: formal and measured — every word counts. Always use: "our clients", "considered advice", "robust approach". Never use: "no win no fee", "cheap", "guaranteed outcome". Email sign-off: "Yours faithfully, Catherine Hart, Senior Partner". Pain points: 1) client care letters are boilerplate and need personalising, 2) internal briefing notes vary in quality, 3) marketing emails feel too informal. They want MULTIPLE Projects — one for Client Communications, one for Internal Documents, one for Marketing Content. Goals: win 2 large commercial property mandates, improve client retention. Tech comfort: high. Device: Windows.
evals.expected_output: Three separate project-instructions files (client-comms, internal-docs, marketing), each under 8000 chars. Setup guide explains all 3 Projects. Knowledge folder with brand summary. Legal compliance (SRA, client confidentiality). QA report covers all 3 instruction files.

assertions.text: Three separate project-instructions files exist (e.g., project-instructions-client-comms.md, project-instructions-internal.md, project-instructions-marketing.md)
Each project-instructions file is under 8,000 characters
No {{PLACEHOLDER}} or [PLACEHOLDER] values remain in any output file
SRA compliance or solicitor regulatory references appear in at least the client communications project instructions
Setup guide explains how to create 3 separate Projects and which instructions/knowledge files go where
All project instructions maintain formal, precise tone appropriate for a law firm
Each project-instructions file includes core brand/business context (not just the use-case-specific part)
QA report validates all 3 project-instructions files (character counts, placeholder checks, consistency)
British English throughout all output files
